work_unit: scripts/parallel-merge.sh — unsafe HEAD@{1} rollback on test failure
situation: audit-2026-07-03-012 finding SCRIPTS-M02 (priority 2, pending): parallel-merge.sh L78 uses `git merge --abort || git reset --hard HEAD@{1}` as fallback when integration test fails after a merge. HEAD@{1} resolves via reflog and can point to an unrelated prior state when the working tree has uncommitted WIP or when reflog has been rewritten, silently destroying in-progress work. Same risk applies to the conflict-path fallback at L89.
action: Capture PRE_MERGE_HEAD=$(git rev-parse HEAD) before attempting merge. On test-failure rollback, prefer `git merge --abort`; only fall back to `git reset --hard $PRE_MERGE_HEAD` if abort is unavailable. Same pattern for the conflict path. Removes dependency on reflog positioning and makes rollback target explicit and stable regardless of working-tree dirtiness.
outcome_evidence: opt-worktree.sh created new direction-shift worktree opt-scripts-shift-1783076394 (public-interface touch detected), committed scripts/parallel-merge.sh with PRE_MERGE_HEAD-based rollback, pushed auto/opt-scripts-shift-1783076394 to origin. Main unchanged; fix awaits human merge review.
next_suggestion: 派生 fix #2: SCRIPTS-L01 (verify-pattern.sh L21 $CAL interpolated in python -c string → use sys.argv)
派生 fix #3: SCRIPTS-L02 (studio-precheck.sh bare except → narrow exception types)
若本 case 被 merge，更新 audit-cycle-state derived_fixes SCRIPTS-M02 status→merged

audit_findings.finding: git reset --hard HEAD@{1} fallback on test-failure rollback can destroy uncommitted WIP or land on wrong commit when reflog has drifted. HEAD@{1} is position-dependent, not content-addressed.
audit_findings.remediation: Pin pre-merge HEAD via $(git rev-parse HEAD) before merge attempt; use that pinned SHA as reset target instead of HEAD@{1}. Prefer merge --abort first; only fall back to reset when abort unavailable.
